**Reasoning:** The Knowles/Meisl nucleation-elongation framework establishes that for sigmoidal amyloid growth: (1) the apparent growth rate scales as a power law of monomer concentration, and (2) seeding bypasses the slow primary nucleation step, shortening the lag/half-time monotonically with seed concentration. The proven 0.9268 winner encodes exactly this: seed-modulated half-time via `c[2]*x1^c3/(1+c6*x2)`, reducing exactly to the parent when x2=0 (unseeded curves unaffected).

My change: adopt the proven 7-constant seed-modulated form. This is the disciplined exploitation move — the parent ignores a known-informative feature (x2). I'll make the half-time both monomer-power-law and seed-modulated, which is the structural principle (seeding accelerates onset) without adding redundant terms.
